110221=1. All alliance members can participate in the Duel. \n2. Once the event theme activates, survivors can teleport to the opposing alliance's location via the event interface. \n3. If the rival alliance is from another State, survivors can return to their Home State through the event interface after teleportation. \n4. During the event, killing units of the opposing alliance grants additional alliance duel points. \n5. After the duel theme ends, all Cross-State survivors will be forcibly returned to their Home States. \n6. During Cross-State duels, teleportation does not consume Teleporters, whereas Teleporters are required for Same-State duels.
110222=Reinforce means dispatching troops to help defend the structure when under attack. \n\n1. You can reinforce your own or allies' structures. \n2. Upgrade the Alliance Center to extend reinforcement cap. \n3. All reinforcements will stand up to fight when the structure is assaulted. \n4. Reinforcements cannot retreat during combat. \n5. When under enemy attack, a portion of grievously wounded units of the reinforcements will perish.

110449=Tile Management Instructions

110451=Declaring War\n1. Attacking a city requires the Alliance leader or an R4 to declare war. \n2. Each alliance can declare war once a day and only on one city at a time\n3. An alliance must have more than 20 members to declare war\n4. The war lasts for 12 hours, during which alliance members can attack the city. Multiple alliances can declare war on the same city.\nAttacking Cities\n1. The city has NPC defenders that survivors must defeat first; if not defeated quickly, the defenders will recover all HP.\n2. After defeating all defenders, survivors need to demolish the city's durability; crews can initiate demolitions to reduce the city's durability every 5 mins. \n3. After demolishing all durability, the ownership of the city is determined based on the points obtained by participating alliances (Points = number of defenders killed * defender points + durability demolished * durability points).\nOccupation Rules\n1. A Level 1 city must be occupied first (the first Level 1 city doesn't need to follow adjacent rules); thereafter, cities declared and attacked must be adjacent to your existing cities' territories.

300797=1. Please ensure you're on a secure network and using a secure device for this operation. \n\n2. Once the account deletion process is completed, you will no longer be able to log in, access or recover your game <color=#de0101>account</color>. Any personal information associated with this account will be deleted or anonymized and cannot be retrieved. \n\n3. After the account deletion process is finished, all virtual currencies, in-game items, and other paid services tied to this game account will also be permanently erased. Please proceed with caution! \n\n4. After submitting the account deletion request, the review process will take approximately <color=#de0101>[15 Days]</color>. During this period, your request will be in the <color=#de0101>[Review]</color> status, and we will conduct a security verification on your request to ensure that the operation was initiated by you, preventing malicious deletion or account theft. You may <color=#de0101>withdraw the request</color> or reach out to customer support to cancel deletion during this period. Please be mindful of the timeframe and proceed with caution. \n\n5. After the review is complete, you can re-enter the account deletion screen to confirm the final account cancellation.

302062=Champion Duel\n Sign-up\n1. Sign-up: Monday to Tuesday. Combat will not result in losses. Heroes and units regular usage will not be affected. \n2. The event is divided into 3 stages: Qualifier, Knockout, and Championship. Top 64 of the Qualifier stage will enter the Knockout stage, and Top 8 emerging from the Knockout stage will enter the Championship stage. \n3. Troop formations can be changed in "My Troop Formation" before the match starts. \n\nQualifier \n1. Wednesday is when the In-State Qualifier begins, a total of 10 rounds of combats will ensue, with an interval of 60 Minutes in between. \n2. Survivors can change troop formations anytime and apply the latest formation to the next match. There will be a 5-minute cooldown before another change. Formations cannot be changed 5 Minutes before each match starts. \n3. 3 accumulated losses will be deemed eliminated. \n4. Top 64 determined by the number of victories will advance to the Knockout stage. (If it's a Cross-State duel, then the Top 64 come from top 32 of both servers.)\n5. When two survivors have the same victory number, the survivor with more points (determined by combat loss) will be deemed eligible for the next stage competition. \n\nKnockout \n1. Thursday is when the Knockout starts. \n2. Top 64 of the Qualifier stage will be divided into 8 groups for the Knockout competition. \n3. There are 3 rounds of matches in the Knockout stage, with an interval of 1 hour in between. After 3 rounds of competition, 1 survivor will advance from each group to enter the final Championship. \n\nChampionship \n1. Top 8 of the Knockout will enter the Championship stage. \n2. Survivors can change troop formations anytime and apply the latest formation to the next match. There will be a 5-minute cooldown before another change. Formations cannot be changed 15 Minutes before each match starts. \n3. Championship includes Round of Top 4, Semi-finals, and Finals, which starts at 12:00 (Game Time) Friday, Saturday and Sunday respectively. \n4. Multiple rounds of combat will ensue, and survivors with more victories will advance. \n\n Combat Attributes\n1. During the event, bonuses to siege attacks and to siege defenses are excluded. \n2. All techs related to combat bonuses will be effective. \n3. During the event, if a survivor's hero attributes are enhanced (i.e. hero upgraded, skill unlocked), tech advanced, structure upgraded or other similar improvements made, then the survivor must go to "My Troop Formation" to save the change in order to make it work on the next match. \n\nRewards\n1. In the Qualifier, a reward can be earned for each victory. The unclaimed rewards will be sent via mail after the entire event is over. \n2. In the Knockout and Championship competition, all survivors will receive rewards based on their ranking. \n3. Knockout and Championship competition rewards will be sent via mail after the event is over. \n

302343=Apocalypse Clash Season \n1. Upon the beginning of the Apocalypse Clash Season, City ownership of the entire apocalyptic continent will be reset, and Survivor's headquarters will re-land on the continent. \n2. Due to geological changes, tons of sustainable resources have appeared on the continent, which are "Sandsouls" and "Sapphires". Both resources can be obtained by capturing the tiles they are on. \n3. Survivors must capture a Lv.1 City before occupying resource tiles. Except for the 1st city, other cities to capture must be connected to our cities and tiles already in control. \n4. Resource tiles are contaminated by virus. Thus, Survivors must boost their virus resistance, otherwise the damage dealt will be greatly reduced. \n5. Survivors can place season buildings on the occupied tiles for diverse buffs which will be removed at the end of the season. \n6. To help survivors dominate the Apocalypse Clash, a fresh batch of heroes has joined the fray. \n7. Survivors will travel somewhere else at the end of the season when the [Sandsouls] and [Sapphires] obtained as well as season buildings built will be removed. On top of that, all heroes' levels and star levels will be reset, with all level-up and star-up materials returned.

302843=Season Mechanism
302844=In Season 3, alliances will compete against counterparts from different States, and you'll have chances to explore those States. 
302845=Glory War
302846=Upon the beginning of the event, an alliance can declare war on another one. \nThe alliance which is declared war upon cannot refuse to fight. \nThe event is divided into 2 phases: the Prep phase and the Battle phase. Alliances can obtain Glory Points by destroying the rival's Alliance Centers and season buildings. \nWhen the event ends, the alliance with higher glory points is deemed victory, and the winning alliance will grab 20% season points from the failing alliance. \nDuring the event, if one side's all alliance centers are destroyed, then that side is deemed defeat.
302847=Alliance Center
302848=Alliance Centers are essential for placing season buildings, so prioritize constructing them. \nThe alliance leader and R4 members are allowed to place down Alliance Centers. \nEach alliance can build up to 3 Alliance Centers, which must be placed on different levels of tiles. \nOnce placed, you can send troops to garrison the center and accelerate its construction. \nAfter salvaged, the Alliance Center's durability will be emptied. Construction resources will be spent again to place it next time. 
302849=Season Buildings
302850=Survivors can build their own season buildings to enjoy rich season stat bonuses. \nSeason buildings must be placed near corresponding Alliance Centers. \nSapphire and Sandsoul tiles on the world map generate sustainable resources once captured. These resources are used for constructing and upgrading season buildings. \nSalvaged season buildings will be in cooldown before they can be placed down again. 
302851=Capture Tiles
302852=Survivors can capture tiles on their own, with higher-level tiles offering greater yields. \nTiles are contaminated by virus. If survivors lack sufficient virus resistance, their troops' attack ability will significantly decrease. \nVirus Lab can boost the resistance, making it easier to capture tiles. \nLand Agency increases the number of tiles we can control. 
302853=Season Rewards
302854=Alliance season rewards are determined by rankings. \nThe rewards will be distributed by the alliance leader. 
302855=Season Reset
302856=Upon the beginning of the new season, hero levels, season resources and season buildings of the previous season will be reset. \nThe ownership of Cities will be reset, allowing alliances to capture Cities again.

302868=Frontline Outpost Instructions
302869=1. Upon the duel starts, alliance leader and R4 members can place a Frontline Outpost on any neutral tile of the rival alliance's State. The Outpost serves as the starting point of land capture, and from which alliance members can capture tiles in that State. \n2. Placing frontline outposts consumes alliance stones, and each alliance can place up to 3 frontline outposts. \n3. Alliance leader and R4 members are allowed to salvage frontline outposts. Salvaged or destroyed frontline outposts will be in a 30-min cooldown before being able to be placed down again.

308003=Alliance Expedition
308004=Form the expeditionary force to destroy rival alliance centers! \n1. The event is divided into 3 phases: Prep, Donation, and Combat. \n\n<color=#5fa3ed>Prep Phase</color>:\n1. The alliance leader and R4 can pick the opponent of the event. \n2. Alliances with similar season points can be selected. \n\n<color=#5fa3ed>Donation Phase</color>:\n1. Both sides donate units to form the expeditionary force. \n2. The more units donated, the more the rewards and the stronger the expeditionary force. \n\n<color=#5fa3ed>Combat Phase</color>:\n1. In the Combat Phase, the alliance leader and R4 members can manually give the defense command. \n2. Once the defense order is issued, the rival expeditionary force will launch attacks against our alliance centers in order starting from alliance center I. \n3. Our side needs to defend against attacks from the rival expeditionary force. \n4. Event points can be obtained for each wave of attack successfully defended. At the end of the event, the side with higher points will be deemed the winner. \n5. The winning side will loot 20% of season points from the losing side.

Every time drawing for discounts will re-activate the shop and refresh the products within. \n4. During the event, refreshing discounts offer chances to receive 10%, 30%, 50%, 70%, or 90% off. \n5. Discount probabilities are as follows: 10% Off - 45%, 30% Off - 30%, 50% Off - 13%, 70% Off - 8%, 90% Off - 4%. \n6. During the event, the 90% Off is guaranteed on the 5th, 9th, 18th, and 32th refresh attempts. \n7. Afer the event concludes, ranking rewards will be sent via mail.

372811=Alliance Intel
372812=Enemy Intel
372813=1. Alliances with at least 20 members are eligible for the Alliance Duel Warm-Up or Duel League. \n2. Each week, your alliance faces one rival in a 7-day duel. Each day has a theme. Performing specific acts earns duel points. The alliance with higher points wins that day. The one with more daily wins takes the weekly victory. \n3. Each duel league lasts 4 weeks. When a new league starts, the qualified alliance will compete against 15 counterparts in the group. \n4. Week 1 matchups are drawn by alliance leaders. Later weeks depend on past results. Final rankings are based on wins and order. \n5. Top alliances may be promoted, while lower ones risk demotion. The number of promotions and demotions is based on actual conditions. Duel League of a higher tier leads to greater daily ranking reward, daily victory reward, weekly tier reward, and league tally reward. Get everybody motivated and fight for a higher tier! \n6. Rewards for the current league are distributed at 01:00 apocalypse time on the 4th week.

372818=Reinforced! 
372819=In-alliance Ranking
372820=1. In the expedition prep phase, Survivors on both sides of the alliance duel can reinforce the expeditionary forces to increase individual reinforcement power and alliance rally power. Reinforced units of higher tiers and bigger volumes grant more reinforcement power. \n2. Once the expeditionary force is assembled, the alliance leader and R4 members can make defense confirmation, after which the rival's expeditionary force will start to attack headquarters of our alliance. Strengths of the rival expeditionary force are correlated to its alliance's rally power. Setters will get certain defense points every time successfully defend a wave of enemy expeditionary attacks and stop being attacked after one failed defense. \n3. The winner of the event is determined by the total defense points earned. The winning alliance will get corresponding points for that week's alliance duel. \n4. When both individual reinforcement power and alliance rally power pass certain benchmarks, reinforcement rewards can be obtained. The higher the reinforcement power, the more tiers of reinforcement rewards can be obtained. \n5. Survivors can collect primary reinforcement rewards directly and can spend expedition medals to unlock advanced rewards. Expedition medals can be obtained by collecting primary reinforcement rewards, completing reinforcement tasks, and buying packs. \n6. In the expedition prep phase, complete random reinforcement tasks to get expedition medals and other rewards. A new reinforcement task spawns every 8 hours.

Donating Toys will upgrade the Tyrant, the higher the Tyrant's level, the greater the rewards. \n3. Toys will be removed when the event's over. Please use them before the donation phase ends. \nWhen the Attack Phase starts, donation will be disabled. \n\nAttack Phase \n1. The alliance leader and R4 members can summon the Tyrant manually. \n2. Then, alliance members can launch attacks against the Tyrant, with Hound Tags required for each attempt. (Canceling an attack won't return the item.) \n3. Deal damage to the Tyrant to get rewards in line with the damage tiers. \n4. At the end of the event, final rewards = the damage tier reward * corresponding reward multiple. \n5. The survivor's event progress will be cleared up upon leaving the current alliance in the attack phase. \nHound Tags will be cleared up when the attack phase is over.

376024=Canyon Clash
376025=Basic Rules
376026=Building Intro
376027=Rewards
376028=Additional Rules
376029=Gameplay
376030=1. The two matched alliances will enter the Canyon to engage in battle. \n2. Initial or sustained occupation of buildings will contribute to Alliance Points. \n3. Capturing buildings, beating opposing survivors, and gathering stones will earn Personal Points. \n4. Escorting Energy Cells to the Alliance Beacon will reward massive Alliance and Personal Points. \n5. Defeating the Canyon Captain grants buffs to all allied members. \n6. After the battle period ends, the side with higher Alliance Points will claim victory. Survivors will receive generous rewards based on Personal Points and the alliance's outcome. 
376031=Schedule
376032=Registration\n1. Voting is open from Monday to Tuesday, with Wednesday for registration, Thursday for matchmaking, and Friday for combat. \n2. Alliances ranked in the Top 20 by power and population reaching 40 can register for the event. \n3. The alliance leader or R4 members can register for the alliance. \n4. Survivors must have been in the alliance for at least 24 hours to participate in the event. \n\nParticipants\n1. The alliance leader and R4 members can select Starters and Substitutes in the Participants interface. \n2. There can be 20 Starters and 30 Substitutes. \n3. Members who have been offline for over 5 days are ineligible for battle or reserve selection. \n\nEnter Battlefield\n1. Starters can enter the battlefield during the prep phase. \n2. If there are absent Starters, Substitutes can enter the battlefield when combat begins. \n3. Before entering the battlefield, survivors must get all injured units healed in the Hospital. \n\nBattle Start\n1. Prep Phase: Starters enter the battlefield and wait for combat to begin. \n2. Battle Phase: \n· Phase I: Field Hospital and Water Refinery are accessible. Capturing them grants battlefield buffs and points. Capturing Quarry also earns points. \n· Phase II: Military Base opens. Capturing it grants massive battlefield points and boosts your side's strengths. \n· Phase III: Energy Station generates Energy Cells. Sending troops to capture and transport them to the Alliance Beacon will instantly grant massive battlefield points. \n\n3. Battle Over: The alliance with higher battlefield points wins. \n\n4. Injury Treatment: Injuries from the Canyon Clash will automatically heal after the battle ends. \n\n5. Reward Distribution: Both personal and alliance rewards will be sent via mail after the battle.
376033=Both alliances respectively spawn in the upper-left and lower-right corners (Red and Blue zones) of the map, where fierce combat shall take place.
376034=Occupying the Field Hospital provides healing support, boosting wound healing speed and significantly lowering healing costs for battlefield combatants. The effect of multiple Field Hospitals stacks.
376035=Occupying the Military Base boosts the overall combat effectiveness of your side, enhancing both attack and defense, which is key to achieving battlefield victory. The effect of multiple Military Bases stacks.
376036=The Energy Station produces an Energy Cell at the 20-minute and 30-minute intervals. Both sides can send troops to capture the Energy Cells. The movement speed of the troop carrying the item will be reduced by 50%. Transporting the Energy Cell to the Alliance Beacon grants 100,000 alliance points to your side.

395424=Merge Rules
395425=1. If the size of an alliance remains small for a long time, the Alliance Merger Program will be triggered, allowing the alliance to merge with another one to improve the alliance strengths significantly. \n2. As the [Applicant], you can send the alliance merge application to the alliance recommended. In return, you can accept an alliance merge application as a [Recipient]. \n3. Once leaders of both alliances reach an agreement, the leader and all members of the [Applicant] side will join the [Recipient]'s alliance, and will instantly teleport to the [Recipient]'s alliance territory. \n4. NOTE: alliance merge is irreversible. The [Applicant] alliance will be disbanded upon the command. Thus, please make your choice carefully!

457526=Trade
457527=Plunder

457529=Rewards
457530=Introduction: \n1. Each Survivor can conduct up to 4 intercity trades per day. Each trade will occupy a truck for a certain period. The more squads unlocked, the more trucks available. \n2. Upon departure, the truck will follow a random route. During the trip, the truck could be looted. No casualties in the truck plunder battles, and each truck can be plundered Twice at most. \n3. When the truck reaches its destination, the trade initiator can collect its remaining cargo. \n\n[Initiate Trade] \n1. Up to 5 heroes can be selected to protect a truck, and each hero can protect only 1 truck at a time. \n2. Once a truck departs, the hero formation cannot be changed. \n3. Each truck has 1 free opportunity to change rewards during the selection phase. \n\n[Trade Rewards] \n1. After a truck reaches its destination, the remaining cargo on the truck can be collected. \n2. Keeping using trade contracts to switch trucks will increase the chances of obtaining high-quality trucks. \n3. Truck quality and the survivor's headquarters level determine the base value of truck cargo. \n4. The more cities occupied by the survivor's alliance, the more cargo the truck can transport additionally - up to 7 extra cargo per truck. \n5. The higher the level of cities occupied by the survivor's alliance, the greater the value of additional cargo. \n\n[Plunder Trucks]\n1. Survivors can loot non-allied trucks on the map up to 4 times daily. \n2. After a successful attack, the attacker can plunder random cargo and resources from the truck, and reduce the truck's integrity by a certain percentage. \n3. Failed looting does not consume plunder chances. \n4. Trucks with integrity below 50% cannot be further looted. \n5. No casualties in the truck plunder battles, and each truck can be plundered twice at most. \n\n[Probability Display] \n1. The spawning probabilities of trucks with different qualities based on the number of refreshes (including free refreshes) are listed as follows: \n(1st refresh): D-grade (50), C-grade (30), B-grade (10), A-grade (5), S-grade (5) \n(2nd Refresh): D-grade (30), C-grade (30), B-grade (20), A-grade (10), S-grade (10) \n(3rd Refresh): D-grade (20), C-grade (20), B-grade (30), A-grade (15), S-grade (15) \n(4th Refresh): D-grade (0), C-grade (20), B-grade (40), A-grade (20), S-grade (20) \n(5th Refresh): D-grade (0), C-grade (0), B-grade (50), A-grade (25), S-grade (25) \n(More than 5 Refreshes): D-grade (0), C-grade (0), B-grade (0), A-grade (0), S-grade (100) \n2. Probability Calculation: If the truck refresh probability is as follows: D-grade 30, C-grade 30, B-grade 20, A-grade 10, and S-grade 10, the chance of getting an S-grade truck is 10%. 
457531=1. Survivors can plunder non-allied trucks while they're in transit on the map. \n2. After the attack succeeds, the attacker randomly loots cargo and resources on the truck. \n3. After being looted, the truck's integrity will decrease in proportion to the resources taken, but it won't drop below 50%. \n4. Trucks with integrity down to 50% cannot be further plundered.
457532=1. The Survivor can select up to 5 heroes to protect the truck before departure. Each hero can only protect 1 truck at a time. \n2. There are no unit losses when fighting looters, and heroes will automatically recover full health after battles. \n3. Each truck can be looted Twice at most.
457533=1. After the truck reaches its destination, the remaining cargo on the truck will be notified to the Survivor initiating the trade via mail. \n2. The base value of the cargo increases in line with the truck's quality and the Survivor's headquarters level. \n3. The more cities the Survivor's alliance occupies, the more cargo the truck can transport additionally - up to 7 extra cargo per truck. \n4. The value of extra cargo depends on the highest level of cities captured by the Survivor's alliance. 
457534=More cities captured, more cargo!

458504=[Participation Requirements] \n1. Your alliance population reaches 20 or more. \n2. You have been in the alliance for at least 24 hours. \n3. Alliance Intercity Trade is available to your State. \n4. Truck Station is already unlocked. \n5. Each alliance can dispatch up to 1 regular truck and 2 express trucks daily. \n6. Only the top 20 alliances in the State can get free regular trucks. \n\n[Truck Acquisition] \n1. Regular Truck: Eeverday at 00:00 State Time, alliances that meet the "Participation Requirements" will be granted a trade truck, and the truck won't enter the prep status until an R4/R5 assigns the truck commander. \n2. Express Truck: When there are no trucks in preparation, you can summon an Express Truck and automatically become the truck commander. \n\n[Truck Prep Phase] \n1. The prep phase lasts for 4 hours, after which the truck will automatically depart for various cities to conduct trade. \n2. Truck Commander: During the prep phase, the truck commander needs to assign escorts to the truck and confirm the cargo to be transported. \n3. Passengers: During the prep phase, passengers need to queue in front of the carriage they wish to board. At the end of the phase, one passenger will be randomly selected from the queue to enter the carriage. \n\n[Truck Travels] \n1. The truck could be attacked by other survivors when traveling, and 3 random slots of cargo will be looted when the attacker wins. \n2. Each truck can be looted up to 3 times. \n\n[Rewards] \n1. Truck Commander: Upon arrival, the truck commander will receive all remaining cargo rewards from the tractor and carriages. \n2. Passengers: Upon arrival, passengers will receive remaining cargo rewards from the carriages.

The grand prize probability for this event is as follows: Regular Round Prize: 8.8%, Treasure Round Prize: 6.7%. Good luck! \n6. 894219=1. Dispatch heroes to perform missions around the world. It takes a certain amount of time to complete a mission, and you will receive corresponding rewards upon completion. \n2. Starred missions cannot be refreshed, as they represent the highest level of rewards. \nThe probabilities of each quality missions are as follows: \nBlue Missions: 68% \nPurple Missions: 24% \nOrange Missions: 8% \n4. There are 5 daily chances to assist allies in collecting their mission rewards, and 5 daily opportunities to plunder mission rewards of non-allies in the world. \n6. Each queue can undertake 2 missions at the same time without occupying marching queues. Unlocking more queues can greatly improve completion efficiency.

483144=During the Capital Clash, occupy the Capital or defeat enemies for points and big rewards!
483145=1. When the Capital is accessible, alliances can dispatch rallied troops over. \n2. Battling around the Capital or garrisoning the Capital will earn Capital Clash points. \n3. Seizing the Capital will increase the capture progress. Once the capture progress reaches 100%, the Capital will belong to the occupying alliance and enter a protection phase. If no alliance achieves 100% capture progress, the Capital's ownership will go to the alliance with the highest capture progress at the end of the competition period. \n4. The leader of the alliance that finally holds the Capital will be able to appoint the President of the State. \n5. The President has privileges such as appointing officials, distributing gifts, issuing policies, etc. \n6. The alliance that captures the Capital will receive powerful buffs. \n7. When the Capital Clash starts for the first time, a point-based reward event will be available. Survivors can earn points and claim rewards by occupying the Capital or eliminating enemies. This point event will not be available in subsequent Capital Clashes.

510181=State Rewards
510182=Event Overview \nThe Cross-State Capital Clash is a 1v1 competition between States, where the winner is determined by capturing the opponent's Capital. \n4-8 States are matched into a group for a 2-3 week battle until a champion State is crowned! \nBecoming the champion is no easy feat—it takes the president's leadership to unite all the state forces! \n\nEvent Rules \nStates within a group are matched randomly, and the winning State advances to the next round, facing other winners until a champion is crowned. \nMatching Rules: Winners are matched with winners, and losers are matched with losers. Victory is achieved by capturing the enemy State's Capital. \n\nEvent Phases \nSunday to Friday: States compete for points by winning in various events to increase their state score. \nInvaders and Defenders: At the end of Friday Apocalypse Time, the side with the higher state score becomes the invader, and the lower-scoring side becomes the defender. \n\nCapital Clash\nStarting Saturday, the invader can teleport to the defender's state to attack their capital and contest ownership. The rules for cross-state capital contests follow the same format: during the contest period, the state that reaches 100% progress successfully occupies the capital. If no state succeeds in capturing the capital by the end of the contest period, the capital belongs to the state with the highest occupation progress. \n\nIf the invader wins, 2 new official positions will be granted. \nOnce the capital is captured, many diamond mines will spawn within its range, and only the winning side can gather them. \nFor survivors within the same state, unity and cooperation are essential! 
510183=Sunday to Friday is the state score competition, where the State with the higher score gains invasion rights.\nOn Saturday, the Invader may enter the enemy State and attempt to seize its Capital. \nThe lower-scoring State is the Defender who needs to defend its Capital on Saturday. \nState points can be earned by completing the following acts:
510184=Saturday [00:00:00] \nInvading survivors can teleport to the Defender's State. \nSaturday [12:00:00] \nThe Capital and Turrets exit protection mode, and the contest phase officially starts. \nSaturday [12:00:00] - [16:00:00] \nCapital contest will determine the winner, and the victorious State will advance to the next round. \nSaturday [24:00:00] \nCross-State enemy buster event ends, and ranking rewards are settled. \n\nCapital Defense Rules \nOnce the Capital Clash starts, the 3 turrets will also become capturable. In battles for the capital and turrets, survivors from the same state are considered part of the same faction, meaning no combat occurs between their troops. For example, after Alliance A from State#1 captures the capital, survivors from Alliance B in State#1, who occupy the turrets, will not attack the capital held by Alliance A. A maximum of 10 troops can be stationed at the capital, and a maximum of 10 troops can be stationed at the turret. Within the Capital zone, attacking other survivors' Headquarters depletes durability TWICE as usual! \n\nTurret Capabilities \nTurrets are essential strategic weapons in the Capital Defense fight: After capturing a turret, it will regularly launch auto-attack on the capital captured by the enemy, killing a lot of defending units stationed there. \n\nSuccessfully Capture the Capital \nThe Capital Defense fight lasts up to 4 hrs. If neither side reaches 100% progress, the state with the highest progress at the end of the countdown wins. The victorious state will proceed to the next round of state duels and match up with another winner until a champion is determined. \n\nState Victory Rewards \nStates that advance successfully will receive victory rewards.

897610=Expedition Duel Started
897611=Alliance admin {0} activated the [Expedition Duel] defense at {1}. Please get ready to fight!

897613=1. During the Expedition Prep Phase, survivors from both sides of the Alliance Duel can donate units to support the expeditionary force in order to increase individual donation power and alliance rally power. The higher the unit level and the more the units donated, the greater the donation power gained. \n2. Once the expedition force is assembled, the alliance leader and R4 members can initiate the defense phase. The enemy expedition force will then begin attacking the headquarters of all our alliance members. The strength of the enemy force will be related to the opposing alliance's rally power. Survivors will earn defense points for each successful defense against an enemy wave, and attacks will cease after two failed defenses. \n3. The outcome of the duel will be determined based on the total defense points accumulated by both alliances. The alliance with the higher total will win, earning points towards this week's Alliance Duel score. \n4. Survivors who meet the required individual donation power and alliance rally power will qualify for donation rewards. The higher the donation power, the more tiers of donation rewards available. \n5. Survivors can claim primary rewards directly from donation rewards and unlock advanced rewards by using Expedition Medals. Expedition Medals can be obtained by claiming primary rewards, clearing donation tasks, and purchasing packs. \n6. During the Prep Phase, random donation tasks can be completed to earn Expedition Medals and other rewards. A new donation task will refresh every 8 hours. \n7. Changing alliances will affect reward eligibility.

550252=Glory War is a competitive event based on alliances, where winning the war can <color=#f07100>loot the rival alliance's season points.</color> At the end of the season, the strongest alliance will be determined. The event will unfold after a certain period into the season. \n\n<color=#f07100>Pre-War Deployment Phase: </color>\n1. In this phase, alliances can choose one of three sessions as a Truce Period. \n2. Once entering the opponent-selection phase or battle phase, the truce period cannot be changed.\n\n<color=#f07100>Target Lock Phase: </color>\n1. The Target Selection phase occurs every <color=#f07100>Monday</color>. \n2. The alliance leader or R4 members must <color=#f07100>select an alliance as the opponent</color>. Once selected, it cannot be changed. \n3. Searching for other opponents costs <color=#f07100>Alliance Stones</color>. \n\n<color=#f07100>War Day and Battle Phases:</color> \n1. War Day occurs every <color=#f07100>Tuesday</color>. If a survivor and their opponent are in different States, the first teleportation via the "Go" button will not consume any Teleporter items. \n2. Battles will occur during the <color=#f07100>earliest available combat session outside the truce period</color> for both alliances. \n3. At least one empty tile around the Alliance Center or season buildings is required to launch attacks. \n\n<color=#f07100>Glory Points</color> \n1. Destroying the opponent's Alliance Center grants <color=#f07100>60,000,000</color> Glory Points. \n2. Destroying rival season buildings earns <color=#f07100>60,000</color> Glory Points. \n3. Draining <color=#f07100>100</color> points of the opposing Alliance Center's durability grants <color=#f07100>400</color> Glory Points. \n4. Reducing <color=#f07100>100</color> points of the opposing season building's durability earns <color=#f07100>50</color> Glory Points. \n5. Defeating <color=#f07100>1</color> enemy unit grants <color=#f07100>1</color> Glory Point. \n6. Each Alliance Center can provide <color=#f07100>25% Glory Point Bonus</color> to its alliance. \n\n<color=#f07100>Victory and Defeat Criteria</color>\n1. During battle phase, if one side <color=#f07100>destroys the opponent's Alliance CenterⅠ</color>, that side immediately wins. \n2. If neither side's Alliance CenterⅠhas been completely destroyed by the end of the battle, the alliance with the <color=#f07100>higher Glory Points</color> wins. \n3. The victorious alliance will <color=#f07100>plunder 20% of the defeated alliance's season points</color> as rewards.

898430=Total Units: 

898432=Event Rules: \n1. Farmlands will randomly spawn every Friday 00:00 in the world. Starting Saturday 00:00, survivors from the same State can deploy troops to gather from them. \n2. Each survivor can only send one troop to one farmland at a time. \n3. Farmlands are secured by guards. Survivors from other States can attack those farmlands. When attacking, they will battle both the guards and any gathering troops within. \n4. If the attacker wins, the attacker will plunder a portion of remaining Food on the farmland, as well as a small share from the gathering troops. \n5. Once all food on a farmland has been plundered or gathered, the farmland will become Expired, preventing further attacks or collection. \n6. The event ends every Sunday 00:00. Once the event concludes, all these farmlands will disappear, and gathering troops will be automatically recalled to their Headquarters.
898433=Event Rules: \n1. During the farmland plundering period, survivors can send troops to other States and loot them. \n2. There is a limit to how much food can be plundered during the event. Once the limit is reached, survivors can continue attacking the farmlands but will not gain additional food. \n3. The number of plunderable food is determined by the survivor's troop load. \n4. Plundered Food = A portion of the Food left on the Farmland + A small amount of Food from the gathering troops. 
898434=Loot Cap: \n1. The number of plunderable food is determined by the survivor's troop load. \n2. Plundered Food = A portion of Food left on the Farmland + A small amount of Food from the gathering troops.
